sn47.13:1.1: This event follows SN 47.12 at Nāḷandā and precedes SN 47.14 at Ukkacelā on the Vajjian shore of the Ganges, so the Buddha must have been somewhere on the road between them, not in Sāvatthī. The commentary rather implausibly tries to reconcile this by proposing that the eighty-year old Buddha and Sāriputta took a side journey of over a thousand kilometres. It is simpler to assume that, since Sāvatthī was the default location for teachings and is often ascribed as a matter of convention, it was mentioned here by mistake.
sn47.13:1.3: The commentaries identify “novice Cunda”, who reported the deaths of Sāriputta (SN 47.13:1.3) and Mahāvīra (DN 29:2.1, MN 104:3.1), as Sāriputta’s younger brother, explaining that the title “novice” was a nickname that persisted from the time he ordained as a young novice. The commentaries further identify him with Mahācunda, one of the great disciples (MN 118:, AN 6.17, MN 8), who later brought the Dhamma to the land of the Cetīs (AN 6.46, AN 10.24), and once stayed with Channa and Sāriputta (MN 144:4.1, SN 35.87:1.2).
sn47.13:2.2: Sāriputta’s passing is treated with simplicity and dignity. There is no mention of relics, or any supernatural occurrences.
sn47.13:5.1: These five “spectrums” (dhammakkhandha) are normally said to be the qualities of arahants (SN 3.24:3.6, AN 10.12:2.2). This is anomalous, as Ānanda is still a trainee and has not fully developed the last two. See also SN 55.26:17.1, where the stream-enterer Anāthapiṇḍika is said to possess right knowledge and right freedom.
sn47.13:5.6: Text’s otiṇṇo appears to be an error and I ignore it.
sn47.13:6.5: “Wear out” and “broke off” are both palujjati.
